news.title: World Cup 2026: Norway 1-2 England (AET) - Jude Bellingham the hero again as Three Lions advance to semi-finals
news.lede: England booked their place in the semi-finals of the World Cup as another double from the brilliant Jude Bellingham clinched a 2-1 extra-time victory over Norway in Miami. Five days after his two-goal heroics against Mexico, Jude Bellingham was England's saviour once again, cancelling out Andreas Schjelderup's opener with a superb finish in first-half stoppage time before grabbing the extra-time winner from close range. The goals take the 23-year-old to six at the tournament, putting him level with Harry Kane and only one behind Erling Haaland, who struggled to make an impact for Norway in a gruelling contest impacted by the hot and humid Florida conditions.

news.title: World Cup 2026 TV schedule: How to watch every knockout game live in the UK
news.lede: The last-32 matches are all free-to-air on the BBC and ITV. From reproductive rights to climate change to Big Tech, The Independent is on the ground when the story is developing. Whether it's investigating the financials of Elon Musk's pro-Trump PAC or producing our latest documentary, 'The A Word', which shines a light on the American women fighting for reproductive rights, we know how important it is to parse out the facts from the messaging. At such a critical moment in US history, we need reporters on the ground.

news.title: 2026 World Cup Predictions
news.lede: I was mildly stressed about having to stay up for a full penalty shootout once Argentina and Switzerland hit extra time, but a Julián Álvarez screamer in the 112th minute bailed out the defending champs for the third straight game. Once again, Argentina looked uninspiring against an opponent that had no business keeping things this close. Switzerland were organized and dangerous enough to equalize through Dan Ndoye in the second half, and even after Breel Embolo's controversial second yellow forced them into a defensive shell for the final fifty minutes, Argentina couldn't find a way through until the game was practically at penalties. That's not particularly encouraging for a team that finally has to face a quality opponent in the semifinal.

news.title: The World Cup all-time table: England 5th, France closing on Italy…
news.lede: From the first tournament in 1930 to the present day, the World Cup has grown into the biggest sporting competition on the planet with billions of people expected to watch some of the 2026 edition. But who are the most successful sides in World Cup history? It won’t surprise anybody to see five-time winners Brazil at the top of our list, but Germany, who have lifted the trophy four times and reached another four finals, aren’t too far behind them despite exiting in the group stages of the last two World Cups. Argentina are third, overtaking Italy thanks to their triumph four years ago.

news.title: World Cup 2026: Argentina 3-1 Switzerland (AET): Julian Alvarez scores stunner to set up blockbuster semi-final vs England
news.lede: Julian Alvarez scored an extra-time stunner as Argentina beat 10-man Switzerland 3-1 and set up a mouth-watering World Cup semi-final clash with England. Lionel Messi and Co will face the Three Lions in Atlanta on Wednesday at 8pm UK time, with a place in the final against either France or Spain on the line. It will be the first competitive meeting between the nations in 24 years. Alexis Mac Allister was twice left unmarked at a corner - and the second time he punished them, by guiding in a header in after being picked out by Messi.
